dimensionDescriptions.identity: Identity and account-centric signals, including account misuse, credential abuse, and authentication patterns.
dimensionDescriptions.endpoint: Endpoint-level artifacts such as host telemetry, process behavior, and local compromise indicators.
dimensionDescriptions.network: Network and access telemetry, including connection behavior, perimeter events, and service exposure.
dimensionDescriptions.intel: External or contextual intelligence used to frame observed indicators and likely adversary behavior.
dimensionDescriptions.timeline: Temporal sequencing and progression of events to assess momentum, causality, and urgency.
dimensionDescriptions.collection: Information-gathering actions that reduce uncertainty before major reporting or response decisions.
dimensionDescriptions.mitigation: Risk-reduction actions intended to contain impact or harden posture.
dimensionDescriptions.detection_gap: Acknowledgment of unknowns, blind spots, or missing telemetry that may affect interpretation.
dimensionDescriptions.tradeoff: Recognition of competing priorities and the cost of acting now versus validating further.
dimensionDescriptions.risk_reasoning: Rationale for stated risk level tied to observed impact, likelihood, and exposure.
dimensionDescriptions.confidence_reasoning: Rationale for confidence level tied to source quality, corroboration, and uncertainty.
dimensionDescriptions.hypothesis_alignment: Clarity about how evidence and choices support the selected working hypothesis.

reportSections.title: Executive Summary
reportSections.template: State your primary hypothesis in one clear sentence, then summarize current risk and confidence in plain language for leadership.

reportSections.title: Situation and Assumptions
reportSections.template: Describe what is known, what is assumed, and which assumptions could materially change your assessment.

reportSections.title: Evidence Summary (3–5 key items)
reportSections.template: Present 3–5 high-value evidence points with brief source context and why each item matters to your hypothesis.

reportSections.title: Hypothesis and Alternatives
reportSections.template: Explain the selected hypothesis and at least one viable alternative interpretation you considered and why it was deprioritized.

reportSections.title: Risk and Confidence Rationale
reportSections.template: Justify your chosen risk and confidence levels using uncertainty, corroboration, and likely impact.

reportSections.title: Collection Plan (top 1–2 actions)
reportSections.template: List the top 1–2 immediate collection actions that would most reduce uncertainty in your current assessment.

reportSections.title: Mitigation Recommendations and Tradeoffs
reportSections.template: Recommend immediate mitigation actions and explain at least one operational tradeoff or downside for each action.

reportSections.title: Professional Conclusion (next steps)
reportSections.template: Close with concise next steps, owners, and decision points for the next reporting cycle.

gapRules.prompt: Consider including a short collection plan that names one or two concrete data sources (such as SSO logs, endpoint telemetry, or email logs) and explains how each would reduce uncertainty.
You may want to note any detection gaps or unknowns so readers can distinguish confirmed findings from unresolved questions.
Consider explaining one tradeoff associated with your recommended action, including potential benefits, costs, or unintended consequences.
Explain how your selected risk level relates to likely impact and probability rather than relying only on the number of indicators observed.
Describe why you selected this confidence level and what additional information would increase your confidence in the assessment.
